visualGoal: Convince a 2026 buyer that direct drive is now the default (price parity with belt) and that 5Nm is enough to start / 9-15Nm is the sweet spot — before they overspend on torque. Real DD base photos across every tier: the cheap entry floor, the sweet spot, the frontier. No procedural art.

realProductPhotosNeeded.notes: The cheapest real DD bundle and the literal proof of the 'price argument is gone' thesis. Currently text + Amazon-search only in the body (tip callout + shortlist table) because no verified image. Needs a real photo before it can appear as a gearpick/shotgrid card or before certification.
Referenced in the torque table (5-8Nm entry tier) and setup-tax Boost-Kit-180 note. Text-only until a real base photo is sourced.
Named in the 12Nm sweet-spot table row alongside the R12. The verified Alpha Evo Pro (18Nm) IS embedded and anchors the Simagic visual; the 12Nm variant is text-only.
Named in the 21-28Nm 'beyond what most can hold' table row. asetek-invicta-pedals.jpg is verified but the BASE image is not; the Asetek visual is anchored on the verified Forte (18Nm) instead.

editorialCompositePrompts.prompt: Realistic Gen X sim-racing garage bench at golden-hour rim light: a rigid 8020 wheel stand with an open 4-bolt mounting plate left empty for a real wheelbase product overlay, an old belt-driven wheel set aside on the workbench in soft focus to imply the upgrade, cable tray visible, curator Duke Alvarez resting a hand on the mount, no fake branded hardware, no fake UI, 16:9 editorial cover.
editorialCompositePrompts.negativePrompt: fake logos, fake wheelbase, warped controls, impossible ergonomics, tiny unreadable text, procedural gradient art

infographicPrompts.title: Direct drive vs belt vs gear: what changed in 2026
infographicPrompts.style: IgnitionSim command-center comparison chart, large readable text, racing colors, real base photos as row markers for Codex overlay

infographicPrompts.title: How much torque you actually need
infographicPrompts.style: Numbered ladder, product-photo placeholders (R3/R9/R12/Evo Pro) for Codex overlays

curatorScenePrompts.prompt: Realistic adult Gen X sim-racing curator Duke Alvarez in a clean garage cockpit bay, one hand on a direct-drive wheelbase mounted to a rigid rig, confident mechanic energy, an entry-tier DD base and a belt wheel staged beside him for the 'do you need it' comparison, product overlay space to one side, practical not fantasy, 4:5 portrait.

openQuestions: Exact current US MSRP for the Cammus C5 (sources span $299 bundle sale / $319.99 wheel / $328 bundle)?
